metadata.title: Build a Plausibility Audit Checklist with Claude: Name the Five Capacities
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: When a build goes wrong, it is not a mystery. It is one of five named failures. The chapter gives you the diagnostic — this video builds the tool that runs it.

Five supervisory capacities: PA, PF, TO, IJ, EI — Plausibility Auditing, Problem Formulation, Tool Orchestration, Interpretive Judgment, Executive Integration. A build log without capacity labels is a narrative. A build log with labels is a diagnostic: each none step is a gap, and gaps are where failures come from.
Build a Python script that reads a build log and produces a supervisory-capacity report: count occurrences, extract context, list unlabeled steps. Standard library only.

props.command: claude "Write a Python script that reads a build log text file and produces a supervisory-capacity report. Look for labels: [PA], [PF], [TO], [IJ], [EI]. For each, count occurrences and extract surrounding sentence as context. Output: markdown table with capacity, count, and one example sentence. Also list any build steps with no capacity label. Standard library only."

beats.narration_text: Read it before trusting it. Argparse for log file and missing flag, regex for label extraction, context extraction, formatted markdown table, unlabeled-step detection.

Script run on 13-step build log: PA 3, PF 1, TO 2, IJ 4, EI 2, none 1 — step 8, gap detected. Capacity table correct against chapter ground truth. Unlabeled step 8 is a pure generation step correctly labeled none.
Add the missing flag that prints only unlabeled steps and suggests which capacity was probably absent.

props.command: claude "Add --missing flag to capacity_audit.py: prints only unlabeled steps and suggests which supervisory capacity was probably absent, based on keyword analysis of the step description."

beats.narration_text: dash-dash missing flag output: Step 8 flagged. Keyword analysis suggests EI was probably absent — logic in a loop-closing step typically requires Executive Integration to verify the loop closes toward the build's long-range goal. Suggestion is specific enough to act on in the next session.

Naming the capacities as they fire is what makes the practice visible, durable, and teachable. The audit script turns the naming habit into a diagnostic artifact: capacity frequency table, unlabeled gaps, suggested missing labels. The missing flag is the tool that closes the loop — from build log to next-session discipline.
Next: research what the bias literature actually says about AI grading.
Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational AI. At NikBearBrown, nikbearbrown dot com.
